channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Xi plantea una ‘estabilidad estratégica constructiva’ para evitar una colisión con EE. UU.
item.description: Xi Jinping se ha pasado el último año haciendo frente al presidente Donald Trump. Ahora, tras dejar clara su posición y establecer a China como par de Estados Unidos, se está enfocando en redefinir su relación.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Lily Kuo

media:content.media:description: El presidente de China, Xi Jinping, recibió al presidente Trump en Pekín el jueves.

item.title: Este truco mental puede ayudarte a hacer más ejercicio
item.description: Tratar de cumplir con la filosofía de “todo o nada” puede impedirte lograr tus metas. Aquí te decimos qué puedes hacer en lugar de eso.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Nicholas Sansone para The New York Times

item.title: La revolución de la IA te saturará de trabajo
item.description: Estamos ante un fenómeno similar a cuando el mundo conoció la lavadora automática: las lavanderas perdieron su trabajo y tú te convertiste en la lavandera.
item.dc:creator: Carl Benedikt Frey

item.title: Trump Says a Top ISIS Leader Was Killed in a U.S.-Nigerian Mission
item.description: The leader, whom the State Department designated a terrorist in 2023, had been hiding in Africa, President Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump announced the operation in a late-night post on Truth Social, his social media platform.

item.title: Latest Weinstein Trial May Have Been ‘Best Shot,’ Former Prosecutors Say
item.description: The Manhattan district attorney said he was considering whether he would ask a jury to consider a rape charge against Harvey Weinstein for a fourth time after a mistrial on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Timothy A. Clary
media:content.media:description: A Manhattan judge declared a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s rape trial on Friday. It was the third time he faced the same charge from the same woman.

item.title: Este reto de 4 semanas te ayudará a dejar el teléfono
item.description: Puede que incluso pases más tiempo al aire libre.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 16, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: What’s on an Octogenarian’s Bucket List? A Tugboat Ride.
item.description: Older Americans are getting a chance to do something they’ve always wanted through programs designed to make dreams — from drinking soda to skydiving — come true.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and James Estrin
item.category: McAllister Towing and Transportation Co Inc

item.title: An Upper West Side Primary Presents a Test of Judaism and Politics
item.description: Stephanie Ruskay is seeking to become the first female rabbi to be elected to state office in New York, but the race may hinge on whether voters find her progressive enough.
item.dc:creator: Maya King

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Ruskay said her faith leadership inspired her to run for office on moral grounds, citing issues including affordability challenges that have pushed people out of the city.

item.title: Trump Turns Taiwan Arms Sales Into Bargaining Chip With China
item.description: The president said a potential arms deal for Taiwan was a “very good negotiating chip” in talks with Beijing. His words raise questions about the steadfastness of U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: Patriot missile systems on display during a Taiwanese military exercise in Taipei last year.

item.title: Long Island Rail Road Strike Shuts Down Busiest U.S. Passenger Rail Service
item.description: This is the first strike on the service in more than 30 years. It comes after three years of failed contract negotiations, two federal interventions and a volley of last-minute bargaining.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:description: The strike is the first on the Long Island Rail Road since 1994.

item.title: It’s Been a Wild Week in British Politics. What Happens Next?
item.description: Nobody has yet challenged Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership, but his leading rival, Andy Burnham, finally has a route to Downing Street.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: Members of the news media congregated outside 10 Downing Street on Thursday, after days of turmoil in the Labour Party over Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s leadership.

item.title: Corrections: May 16, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, May 16, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: From TV to T.P., Why Your Life Is Subscriptions
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, May 16, 2026.

item.title: The Soprano Rainelle Krause's Death is a Loss of More Than Just a Role
item.description: The coloratura soprano Rainelle Krause, who died in March, was one of the most sought after performers of Queen of the Night in ‘The Magic Flute.’
item.dc:creator: David Belcher

item.title: Two Killed as Car Slams Into Crowd on Sidewalk in Manhattan
item.description: A black Mercedes S.U.V. smashed into two parked cars, crossed a bike lane and crashed into a crowd of people, coming to a stop against a parking meter.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A black Mercedes-Benz slammed into a sidewalk on the Upper West Side on Friday, striking a group of people and parked vehicles. At least two bystanders were killed.

item.title: Triumphs Smoothly
item.description: Byron Walden’s latest themeless puzzle is a tough torrent.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Sometimes solvers just need to go with the flow, but also pick the right vehicle.

item.title: Plot Was ‘Targeting Heart’ of New York’s Jewish Community, Tisch Says
item.description: Police Commissioner Jessica S. Tisch spoke after prosecutors said they had arrested a man planning to attack a Manhattan synagogue.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer and Maia Coleman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Commissioner Jessica S. Tisch spoke on Friday at Temple Emanu-El in Manhattan, hours after federal authorities said they disrupted a plot against a synagogue.

item.title: Catholic Clergy Can Minister Within Illinois ICE Facility After Legal Agreement
item.description: Several clergy members and an advocacy group had sued the Trump administration, arguing that they were unlawfully denied access to detainees at an Illinois facility.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers prevented religious leaders from entering the ICE facility in Broadview, Ill., and offering communion to detainees last November.

item.title: EE. UU. contempla la posible imputación de Raúl Castro
item.description: En medio de un aumento de los vuelos de vigilancia, una visita del director de la CIA y un embargo energético, la Casa Blanca intenta aumentar la presión sobre Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Michael Crowley and Frances Robles
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Raúl Castro es expresidente de Cuba y hermano menor de Fidel Castro.

item.title: Why Were These C.E.O.s in Beijing With Trump?
item.description: Some of America’s most powerful C.E.O.s accompanied President Trump to Beijing during his summit with President Xi Jinping of China. Our reporter Ana Swanson explains what they were hoping to gain from the trip.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov and James Surdam

item.title: OpenAI Bought Company That Offered A.I. Tools for Cloning Voices
item.description: The acquisition, Weights.gg, was a sort of social network for creating and sharing artificial intelligence algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The San Francisco offices of OpenAI, which backed away from earlier efforts to clone voices with artificial intelligence.

item.title: Top Drug Regulator Is Fired From the F.D.A.
item.description: Dr. Tracy Beth Hoeg said she did not know who had fired her or why, but the dismissal came amid tumult at the agency in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Apoorva Mandavilli

media:content.media:credit: Anne Chadwick Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. Tracy Beth Hoeg’s hiring had startled public health leaders familiar with her track record as a vaccine skeptic. But she defended her record at the Food and Drug Administration on Friday, saying she “stuck with the science.”

item.title: F.A.A. Says Better Scheduling Can Address Air Traffic Control Staffing Issues
item.description: In a new report, the F.A.A. says that by increasing controllers’ active work hours per shift, it could reduce its target for a fully staffed work force by over 2,000 positions.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The air traffic control tower at Newark Liberty International Airport in Newark, N.J. For years, controllers have been complaining of exhaustion, fatigue and low morale.

item.title: Wait, Is That Poison Ivy? A Quiz.
item.description: Test your ability to spot poison ivy and oak before they give you a rash.
item.dc:creator: Erik Vance

item.title: Cuba, un país a oscuras
item.description: Estados Unidos cortó el suministro de combustible a Cuba, sumiendo a la ya empobrecida isla en una aguda crisis energética.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Varias personas juegan al dominó con una linterna mientras arde una hoguera de protesta en La Habana el jueves.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Virginia Democrats’ Effort to Reinstate New Voting Map
item.description: State officials had asked the justices to step in to allow the state to use a congressional map in the midterms that was drawn by Democrats and recently approved by voters.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court’s ruling blocking the map wipes out four newly drawn Democratic-leaning House districts in Virginia.

item.title: Hospital Will Open First ‘Detransition’ Clinic in Legal Settlement With Texas
item.description: Texas Children’s Hospital was under investigation for billing practices on gender-transition treatments. The settlement was expected to end that inquiry.
item.dc:creator: Amy Harmon

media:content.media:credit: Melissa Phillip/Houston Chronicle, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Texas Children’s Hospital in Houston is one of the largest children’s hospitals in the country.

item.title: Clavicular Gets Plea Deal After Alligator Shooting in Florida
item.description: The social media personality pleaded no contest and was sentenced to six months of probation. The deal allows him to avoid jail time.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Braden Peters, better known by his online name Clavicular, has been arrested in Florida and Arizona this year.

item.title: Smithsonian Adds Back Impeachment Language to Label on Trump Portrait
item.description: The language had been removed from wall text in the National Portrait Gallery, but it’s back as the museum unveiled changes to its exhibition on U.S. presidents.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small and Deborah Lynn Blumberg

media:content.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The wall text accompanying President Trump’s image in the National Portrait Gallery has been adjusted again.

item.title: The Push to Indict Raúl Castro, Cuba’s Former President
item.description: Mr. Castro, Cuba’s former president, could soon face charges in the 1996 killings of four volunteer airmen who were members of a humanitarian group that searched for migrants at sea.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: Former President Raúl Castro of Cuba attending a May Day parade in Havana this month.

item.title: Ex-Texas Lottery Commission Leader Charged in Guaranteed Jackpot Scheme
item.description: Prosecutors in Travis County, Texas, filed charges against Gary Grief and the disbanded Texas Lottery Commission in connection with a 2023 plan that arranged a surefire jackpot.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Texas Lottery kiosk at a Chevron food mart and gas station in Fort Worth, Texas.

item.title: Arqueólogos hallan un fragmento de la ‘Ilíada’ enterrado con una momia egipcia
item.description: Para los difuntos del Egipto de la época romana, la literatura griega puede haber ofrecido una clave para una vida en el más allá más cómoda.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Attacks on Russian Apartment Building Kills 4
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck an apartment building and ignited a fire at an oil refinery in the Russian city of Ryazan on Friday. At least four people were killed in the attack on the residential building, according to the regional governor. The barrage follows a series of Russian drone strikes that killed at least 24 people in Kyiv, Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Monika Cvorak, Jorge Mitssunaga and Nataliya Vasilyeva

item.title: Debris From Overpass Strikes Car on Busy N.Y.C. Road
item.description: Dashcam video caught the moment chunks of concrete and debris fell onto a car on the Trans-Manhattan Expressway. Port Authority officials say they are investigating the cause of the incident.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

item.title: Iran-Backed Commander Accused of Plotting U.S. Attacks
item.description: Also, the Eurovision finale. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Jack Douglas, Producer for Aerosmith and Lennon, Dies at 80
item.description: A onetime Beatlemaniac, he helped shape the sound of hits like “Walk This Way” and “(Just Like) Starting Over.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

media:content.media:description: Jack Douglas, center, the producer of Aerosmith’s 1977 album “Draw the Line,” during a recording session with the engineer Jay Messina, left, and Steven Tyler, the band’s lead singer.

item.title: Don’t Run Away From Fear. ‘Agree’ With It.
item.description: What does it mean to “make friends” with fear? On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the beloved Buddhist teacher and author Pema Chödrön explores how to embrace uncertainty and loss, rather than avoiding them.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: You Should Sit With Boredom
item.description: Social media and smartphones have chipped away at our ability to tolerate stillness and boredom. But what gets lost in a world of constant distraction? On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the Buddhist teacher Pema Chödrön argues that the world becomes more alive in stillness.

item.title: Claudine Longet, Entertainer Who Shot Olympian Boyfriend, Dies at 84
item.description: A singer and actress, she drew wide attention for the fatal 1976 shooting of Spider Sabich. She was convicted of negligent homicide.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

item.title: Will the Iran War Backlash Save Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky?
item.description: A view from Thomas Massie’s district in Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky

item.title: Ahead of U.S. 250th Anniversary, More National Guard Troops to Arrive in D.C.
item.description: Federal officials say that hundreds of additional agents, officers and National Guard troops will be deployed ahead of the nation’s 250th birthday celebration.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson
item.category: US Federal Takeover of Metropolitan Police Dept (DC)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the Mississippi National Guard near the Washington Monument in April.

item.title: With Possible Raúl Castro Indictment, U.S. Eyes Venezuela Playbook
item.description: Amid stepped-up surveillance flights, a visit of the C.I.A. director and an energy embargo, the White House is trying to increase pressure on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Michael Crowley and Frances Robles
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Raúl Castro is the former president of Cuba and younger brother of Fidel Castro.

item.title: Tina Peters, Colorado Election Denier, Will Be Freed by Gov. Jared Polis
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis, a Democrat, commuted the sentence of Ms. Peters, a former county clerk serving a nine-year sentence for her role in a plot to examine voting machines after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy and Nick Corasaniti

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tina Peters, the former clerk of Mesa County, Colo., was sentenced to nine years in prison in 2024 after being found guilty of tampering with voting machines.

item.title: Navy Aircraft Carrier to Complete Longest Deployment Since Vietnam
item.description: After being diverted for combat with Venezuela and Iran, the U.S.S. Gerald R. Ford will return to Norfolk, Va., on Saturday after nearly a year at sea.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The U.S.S. Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier arrives at Souda Bay naval base on the island of Crete, Greece, in March.

item.title: Falling Chunks of Debris Are Endangering Drivers on a Busy N.Y.C. Road
item.description: After two incidents, crews made repairs and installed protective netting on the Trans-Manhattan Expressway, which leads to the George Washington Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

item.title: Fugitive Accused of Killing Punk Rock Singer in 1993 Is Arrested in Panama
item.description: Richard Werstine was detained in Panama City last month after more than 30 years on the run, according to the authorities. He is accused of murdering Rodney Barger of the Detroit band Cold as Life.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:description: Richard Werstine, 56, center, is in Texas on his way to Detroit to face second-degree murder and felony firearms charges, according to prosecutors.

item.title: Photos: Cuba Plunges Into Darkness as U.S. Cuts Off Oil Supply
item.description: The United States has choked off Cuba’s fuel supply, plunging the already impoverished island into an acute energy crisis.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: People playing dominoes by flashlight as a protest fire burns in the background in Havana on Thursday.

item.title: NPR Podcast Host Ramtin Arablouei Exits Amid Workplace Inquiry
item.description: Ramtin Arablouei, a co-host of “Throughline,” left the network after an employee made a human resources complaint about his behavior.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: NPR studios in Washington, D.C.

item.title: Inside the Secret History of the DeGrange Family
item.description: One fateful decision 100 years ago created parallel lives. How does a family broken by the bizarre rules of racism heal itself after three generations apart?
item.dc:creator: Susan Saulny

media:content.media:credit: Camille Farrah Lenain for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Susan Saulny, granddaughter of George DeGrange, and Christine DeGrange and Laura Oswald, granddaughters of Edward DeGrange, at the building that formerly held the Sisters of the Holy Family convent.
